title: Deepak Chopra Three Dosha
poses: Action: Press-and-release massage from head to arms. Focus: Move toward the heart; awaken circulation.
Action: Massage belly, back, and legs. Focus: Firm pressure upward; move energy to the heart.
Action: Hug knees to chest; rock side to side. Focus: Massage the lower back; keep neck free.
Action: Inhale, rise to kneeling; Exhale, sit on heels. Focus: Fluid, slow movement; vertical spine.
Action: Fold over one straight leg. Focus: Reach through the heel; lengthen from the lower back.
Action: Lift legs and hips; support back with hands. Focus: Stillness in the throat; gaze at toes.
Action: Lower feet over head. Focus: Stretch through heels; do not strain the neck.
Action: Lie on belly; lift chest. Focus: Use back muscles; expand the ribs.
Action: Lift legs and chest off floor. Focus: Lengthen the spine; breathe into the belly.
Action: Sit and twist toward the upright knee. Focus: Twist on the exhale; keep chest open.
Action: Stand and fold forward. Focus: Gravity pulls the spine long; knees relaxed.
Action: Lie flat on back; palms up. Focus: Total surrender; let the body absorb the practice.
Action: Alternate nostril breathing. Focus: Natural rhythm; balance the left and right brain.

title: Surya Namaskara Sequence (Ashtanga method)
poses: Action: Exhale, bring palms to heart center. Focus: Settle the breath; find stillness.
Action: Inhale, sweep arms overhead. Focus: Lift ribs; look slightly up.
Action: Exhale, fold forward. Focus: Relax neck; keep knees soft; hands to floor.
Action: Inhale, step Left (right) leg back, knee down. Focus: Open chest forward; keep front knee over ankle.
Action: Exhale, step Right (left) leg back to Down Dog. Focus: Lift hips high; press floor away.
Action: Exhale, drop knees, chest, and chin. Focus: Eight points on the floor; elbows in.
Action: Inhale, slide forward and lift chest. Focus: Shoulders down; open the heart.
Action: Exhale, lift hips back to Down Dog. Focus: Stretch heels down; lengthen spine.
Action: Inhale, step Right (left) leg forward, back knee down. Focus: Sink hips; lift heart.
Action: Exhale, step Left (right) leg forward and fold. Focus: Let the head hang heavy.
Action: Inhale, sweep arms overhead to stand. Focus: Reach through fingertips.
Action: Exhale, palms to heart. Focus: Settle the breath; acknowledge the sun.

title: Heart Trouble (Restorative)
poses: [Note: Do not strain. Rest if tired.]

title: Weekly Practice Day 2
poses: Ujjayi Pranayama (Section 203) or Nadi Sodhana Pranayama  (Section 205) for 8 minutes in any comfortable asana

title: Week 16 & 17
poses: Do Ujjayi Pranayama (#203) without inhalation retention in Siddhasana.

title: Weekly Practice Day 1
poses: half a minute on right side
Nadi Sodhana Pranayama in Padmasana (104) or in Virasana (89) or in Siddbasana (84) with inhalation retention for 10 minutes

title: Weekly Practice Day 3
poses: Ujjayi Pranayama (Section 203) or Nadi Sodhana Pranayama (Section 205) without Kumbhaka or retention

title: Week 31 to 35
poses: All the Padmasana Cycle can be done at a stretch
Nadi Sodhana Pranayama (Section 205) without inhalation retention for 10 minutes

title: Week 45 to 50
poses: Stay for 5 minutes then the rest of the Sirsasana variations are done in a stretch

title: Week 55 to 60
poses: Urdhva Dhanurasana II for 12 times in technique II

title: Weekly Practice Day 2
poses: Urdhva Dhanurasana I for 12 times in technique I

title: Weekly Practice Day 3
poses: Urdhva Dhanurasana I for 15 times in technique I

title: Weekly Practice Day 4
poses: Urdhva Dhanurasana I for 12 times staying 20 seconds each time

title: Weekly Practice Day 5
poses: Urdhva Dhanurasana I for 15 to 20 times

title: Final Practice Day 1
poses: Finish each asana 488-499 with Viparita Chakrasana

title: Final Practice Day 3
poses: Sirsapadasana and Ganda Bherundasana, Viparita Salabhasana, and Tiryang Mukhottanasana all done together

title: Week 79 to 84
poses: Sit in Siddhasana (84) or VIrasana (89) or Padmasana (104) or Baddha Konasana (102).

title: Week 109 to 125
poses: Work towards practising this 15 times a day

title: Week 276 to 300
poses: Ganda Bherundasana and Viparita Shalabhasana together (580, 581, and 584) in one stretch, then go to Urdhva Dhanurasana (486) to do Tiryang Mukhottanasana (586).

title: Weekly Practice Day 5
poses: All these balancing asanas at one stretch

title: Weekly Practice Day 6
poses: One minute on each way changing legs

title: Day 1
category: How to Use Yoga by Mira Mehta > Week 1
